steps.title: Combine the radicals using the multiplication rule
steps.content:
Apply the property $\sqrt{x} \times \sqrt{y} = \sqrt{xy}$:
$$\sqrt{(a + c)^3} \times \sqrt{a + c} = \sqrt{(a + c)^3 \times (a + c)}$$
Combine the exponents inside the radical:
$$= \sqrt{(a + c)^{3+1}} = \sqrt{(a + c)^4}$$

steps.title: Simplify the fourth root
steps.content:
Since $a > 0$ and $c > 0$, we know $(a + c) > 0$. Therefore:
$$\sqrt{(a + c)^4} = (a + c)^2$$

steps.title: Expand the squared binomial
steps.content:
Use the binomial formula $(x + y)^2 = x^2 + 2xy + y^2$:
$$(a + c)^2 = a^2 + 2ac + c^2$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
